content:
- Received PO email request: "Use continue-ralph skill to stop and start loop" and followed it directly.
- Sent frequent email updates to PO throughout execution (ack, plan, subtask starts/completions, final summary).
- Read continue-ralph skill and executed steps in order:
  1) checked ralph service (active) and stopped it,
  2) git add/cleanup,
  3) committed as 0352c6a (auto-commit before ralph continue),
  4) ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully,
  5) restarted via timeout --signal=QUIT 5s /bin/bash .ralph/ralph.sh || true and detached.
- Observed transient systemd path warnings during startup output, but worker started and iteration 229 began; final service state verified active.
- Verified process manager state with ../receive_mail/agent-status.sh showing iron-cobra running.
- No code fixes were performed beyond required continue-ralph workflow actions.
- Next if asked: investigate/cleanup recurring transient systemd path warnings during ralph start, but only if explicitly requested.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and searched for failing/meta tasks.
- Confirmed no bug tasks are currently failing (<passes>false</passes> / <passing>false</passing> not present under bugs).
- Found multiple non-bug tasks still <passes>false</passes>, including two ultra-high-priority tasks in story-rust-system-harness.
- Chose the first ultra-high task in that story chain: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/40-task-ultra-high-prio-test-target-split-and-reference-migration.md
- Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next: start task 40 work (migrate repo gates to make test + make test-long only), then follow chain to task 41.
